title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this PwC replay episode (kickoff of the Reporting reset series) reviews foundational financial statement presentation topics, including GAAP and SEC requirements, public vs. private differences, and accounting changes/error corrections. The episode walks through determining reporting periods, balance sheet and income statement presentation, and subsequent events, with timestamps and links to relevant chapters in PwC’s Financial statement presentation guide.
bullets: Foundational GAAP and SEC presentation requirements (1:25)
Determining appropriate reporting periods (3:09)
Balance sheet: classification, required disclosures, and best practices (5:25)
Income statement: structure and key considerations (11:44)
Accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and subsequent events (21:31; 29:53)
takeaways: Ensure balance sheet classification and disclosures accurately reflect capital assets, leases, and liabilities—this affects financing and liquidity assessments.
Be mindful of reporting period determination and recognition timing, as these influence budgeting and project revenue/expense timing.
Monitor accounting changes, estimates, error corrections, and subsequent events for potential impacts on covenants, financings, or stakeholder reporting.
Refer to the linked PwC chapters for detailed presentation and disclosure requirements to support consistent, audit-ready reporting.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description, DeepKeep on Google Cloud is a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions, offering total visibility to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats. It highlights AI Lens for visibility and policies, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing, and an AI Firewall that secures prompts and responses in real time.
bullets: Unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions.
AI Lens: visibility and policy enforcement across user-AI interactions.
AI Red Teaming: stress-test applications and models against real-world attacks.
AI Firewall: real-time prompt/response protection with built-in guardrails.
takeaways: Total visibility helps identify and eliminate shadow AI to reduce unmanaged AI activity on your infrastructure.
Built-in red teaming lets teams validate resilience of models and applications before production deployment.
Real-time firewall and guardrails provide a layer of runtime protection for prompts and responses.
A unified solution can simplify governance and security controls across AI agents and enterprise applications.

items.title: On the Edge: World Cup Ski Racing | Ryan Cochran-Siegle

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this video profiles U.S. ski racer Ryan Cochran-Siegle and highlights his competitive mindset with the line "Nothing is given, everything is earned." It is part of ESPN Originals' On the Edge: World Cup Ski Racing and directs viewers to watch at teamusa.com/tv and follow Team USA across multiple social platforms.
bullets: Profiles Ryan Cochran-Siegle and his Olympic-family legacy and work ethic.
Episode from ESPN Originals: On the Edge: World Cup Ski Racing.
Key tagline: "Nothing is given, everything is earned."
Available on teamusa.com/tv and Team USA's YouTube channel.
Description includes links to Team USA social accounts (Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, TikTok).
takeaways: Ensure streaming and hosting capacity for teamusa.com/tv and YouTube to support episode releases and on-demand playback.
Configure CDN and cross-platform delivery to handle linked distribution across Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and TikTok.
Provision media asset management and archival storage for ESPN Originals content to support repeat viewing and promotional use.
Implement analytics and monitoring for multi-channel engagement driven by the description's social links.

items.title: Lions, Koalas, & GPUs: Optimizing AI Inference

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The video uses a zoo analogy to show how traditional load balancers that treat all AI inference requests the same lead to wasted GPU resources, long waits, and overloaded hardware. It presents Google Cloud’s GKE Inference Gateway as a "smart zookeeper" that optimizes routing and load balancing for specialized compute like TPUs and GPUs.
bullets: Traditional load balancers treat every inference request the same, causing inefficiency.
Uniform handling leads to GPU underutilization, higher costs, and increased latency.
GKE Inference Gateway is presented as a targeted solution for AI/LLM workloads.
The gateway optimizes routing and load balancing for specialized hardware (TPUs/GPUs).
takeaways: Differentiate inference traffic—one-size-fits-all load balancing wastes expensive accelerators.
Evaluate GKE Inference Gateway to improve GPU/TPU utilization and reduce cloud costs.
Match workloads to appropriate hardware to lower latency and avoid overloaded accelerators.
Integrating inference-aware routing on Kubernetes can make AI serving more efficient and predictable.

bullets: No transcript available; summary derived from the description.
Description: "The sky is full of stars, and Copilot helps Christa find the star within."
Uses a starry, inspirational metaphor to position Copilot as empowering.
Clear call to action: download and try Copilot via the link in bio.
takeaways: Promotional messaging highlights user empowerment—evaluate how Copilot’s presentation might affect adoption among staff.
Direct call to action (download link) makes it easy to trial the product—consider running a hands-on pilot.
Content is consumer-facing and inspirational—assess user experience and training needs if considering broader deployment.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, GeForce Garage presents the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build — u/JeffersonPutnam’s small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The video highlights the build’s key parts including a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 rig from the Summer of RTX community contest.
Host: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato).
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker: https://pcpartpicker.com/list/hxnCRV.
Key components: Fractal Design Era 2, RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000.
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 SFX PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
takeaways: A flagship RTX 5090 Founders Edition was integrated into a compact Fractal Era 2 chassis, demonstrating installation of high-performance GPUs in SFF form factors.
The build pairs the RTX 5090 with a 1000W SFX power supply (Corsair SF1000), indicating substantial power provisioning is required for top-tier consumer GPUs.
Active liquid cooling (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO) is used alongside the Ryzen 7 9800X3D, highlighting the cooling considerations when combining high-power CPU and GPU in constrained volumes.
Motherboard choice (MSI MPG B850I Edge) and component compatibility are key for small-form-factor systems using high-end parts; verify layout and thermal clearances when planning dense deployments.
